featureName: BUG-021-007: resurfacing worthiness must be LLM-driven, not a hardcoded dormancy/relevance window
reporter: owner directive (no const limits; LLM decides per situation)

executionHistory.summary: After BUG-021-006 made alert timing LLM-driven, an audit of sibling hardcoded business thresholds found the resurfacing pipeline's dormancy strategy (Engine.Resurface Strategy 1) still decided 'worth resurfacing?' with a hardcoded window: last_accessed < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days' AND relevance_score > 0.3. This answers the same domain question docs/smackerel.md §3.6 requires to be LLM-driven (worthiness depends on the item: a reference returned to often before going quiet vs something opened once; relevance 0.31 vs 0.29 is not categorically worth/not-worth). Serendipity (random rediscovery) carries no worthiness threshold and was scoped out.

Single bugfix-fastlane scope reusing the BUG-021-006 pattern: one resurface_evaluate scenario + BridgeResurfaceEvaluator (mockable) + a gatherResurfaceCandidates retrieval helper; Strategy 1 retrieves dormant candidates within an operational dormancy-retrieval floor and lets the LLM judge each; operational bounds (min_dormancy_days, max_candidates, confidence_floor) to fail-loud SST. SCN-021-RESURFACE-01..03 authored.

Created config/prompt_contracts/resurface-evaluate-v1.yaml. internal/intelligence/resurface_eval.go: ResurfaceSignals (ArtifactID json:"-"), ResurfaceDecision, ResurfaceEvaluator + BridgeResurfaceEvaluator, ResurfaceConfig, resurfaceShouldSurface, noop_resurface_evaluate init(). Engine gained a resurface field + SetResurfaceConfig. Reworked Engine.Resurface Strategy 1 to retrieve dormant candidates via the new gatherResurfaceCandidates helper (COALESCE(last_accessed,created_at) < NOW() - make_interval(days => $min_dormancy_days), capped by $max_candidates) and route each through the LLM, gating on the confidence floor; graceful skip when nil. Removed the INTERVAL '30 days' + relevance_score > 0.3 dormancy window. Serendipity unchanged. SST: intelligence.resurface.* (smackerel.yaml + config.sh + internal/config/resurface.go). Wiring: wireResurfaceEvaluator + main.go call.

resurface_eval_test.go: scripted runner proves parse, scenario routing, public-signal forwarding, internal-field (ArtifactID) non-leak, all error paths + the pure gate. config/resurface_test.go: SST populate + fail-loud + range. scenario-lint registers resurface_evaluate (12 registered, up from 11); go build ./... + vet green.

Full intelligence + config + scheduler + cmd/core + scenario-lint + agent packages green. ./smackerel.sh config generate resolves the new keys into dev.env. Existing resurface tests (serendipity, MarkResurfaced, ResurfaceCandidate fields) unaffected — Strategy 1's struct construction no longer sets LastAccessed in the dormancy path but those tests assert serendipity/MarkResurfaced/field semantics, not the dormancy query.

One scenario + one evaluator + one gatherResurfaceCandidates helper; the worthiness decision and the human-facing reason share a single path. No duplication with the cooling/alert-timing evaluators (each is its own bounded contract).

gatherResurfaceCandidates collects all candidates before any LLM call (no DB cursor held during Invoke); the Resurface loop honors limit and context cancellation mid-iteration; the scenario carries operational LLM-call bounds. Nil-evaluator path degrades gracefully to serendipity-only.

ResurfaceSignals.ArtifactID is json:"-" and proven (by test) not to enter the LLM envelope; only title + day-count + relevance + access-count are sent. The candidate query is parameterized (no SQL injection; make_interval(days => $1) + LIMIT $2). No framework files touched.
artifact-lint PASSED for the packet; config generate + build + vet + affected packages green. Evidence in report.md.

No hardcoded dormancy worthiness window remains (relevance_score > 0.3 removed; the single remaining INTERVAL '30 days' at line 305 is the serendipity calendar-context query, operational, intentionally left). No migration; generated env gitignored; no .github/bubbles changes; parent spec 021 planning artifacts untouched.

Scenario YAML, resurface_eval.go, resurface.go, smackerel.yaml, and wiring document the LLM-driven judgment and the operational/business boundary. The packet records continuation of BUG-021-006 and notes serendipity as intentionally non-deterministic (not threshold-judged).

scopeProgress.title: LLM-driven resurfacing worthiness for the dormancy strategy

lockdownState.reason: bugfix-fastlane LLM-driven rework; no convergence loop required

resolvedFindings.summary: Engine.Resurface Strategy 1 decided 'worth resurfacing?' with a hardcoded dormancy + relevance window (INTERVAL '30 days', relevance_score > 0.3), contradicting docs/smackerel.md §3.6 (domain reasoning must be LLM-driven) and the owner directive.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Moved the worthiness judgment to the resurface_evaluate LLM scenario via BridgeResurfaceEvaluator; the strategy retrieves dormant candidates within an operational dormancy-retrieval floor only; operational bounds moved to fail-loud SST. No hardcoded worthiness window remains; serendipity unchanged.
resolvedFindings.evidence: report.md Test/Validation Evidence; internal/intelligence/resurface_eval.go; config/prompt_contracts/resurface-evaluate-v1.yaml; relevance_score > 0.3 removed from Strategy 1.
